openmeter/watermill/driver/kafka/metrics.purpose: Bridges go-metrics (used by Sarama/Kafka clients) to OpenTelemetry by wrapping go-metrics types so every metric mutation is forwarded to an otelmetric.Meter — exposing Kafka client telemetry in the OTel pipeline without changing Sarama internals.

patterns.name: Wrap-and-delegate metric types
patterns.description: Each wrapped type (wrappedMeter, wrappedCounter, wrappedGauge, wrappedGaugeFloat64, wrappedHistogram) embeds the original go-metrics interface and overrides only mutation methods (Mark, Inc, Dec, Update, Record) to call both the OTel instrument and the underlying go-metrics method.
patterns.example: func (m *wrappedCounter) Inc(n int64) { m.otelMeter.Add(context.Background(), n, otelmetric.WithAttributeSet(m.attributes)); m.Counter.Inc(n) }

patterns.name: Registry delegation via embedding
patterns.description: registry embeds metrics.Registry for all read/list operations and overrides only Register and GetOrRegister to intercept and wrap new metric registrations.
patterns.example: type registry struct { metrics.Registry; mu sync.Mutex; meticMeter otelmetric.Meter }

patterns.name: NameTransformFn for renaming + drop filtering
patterns.description: All metric names pass through nameTransformFn before OTel instrument creation; returning Drop:true bypasses wrapping and returns the raw go-metrics type unchanged.
patterns.example: transfomedMetric := r.nameTransformFn(name); if transfomedMetric.Drop { return def, nil }

patterns.name: Mutex-guarded registration
patterns.description: GetOrRegister and Register acquire r.mu before reading/writing the embedded Registry, preventing races on concurrent Sarama metric registration.

patterns.name: Reflect-based factory unwrapping
patterns.description: def may be a zero-arg function returning the metric (Sarama pattern); reflect.ValueOf(def).Kind()==reflect.Func triggers a call to extract the metric before the type switch.
patterns.example: if v := reflect.ValueOf(def); v.Kind() == reflect.Func { def = v.Call(nil)[0].Interface() }

patterns.name: context.Background() for OTel calls (documented limitation)
patterns.description: All OTel instrument calls use context.Background() because go-metrics mutation methods carry no context. This is an accepted limitation documented in README.md — one of the few legitimate exceptions to the no-Background rule.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: NewRegistry constructor, registry, wrapped metric structs and their OTel-forwarding mutation methods, getWrappedMeter type switch.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new go-metrics type needs (1) a new wrapped struct embedding it, (2) a case in getWrappedMeter, (3) overrides of every mutation method — missing any leaves silent gaps where OTel receives no data.

key_file_guides.role: Documents known limitations: context is always context.Background() and registration errors are only logged, never propagated.
key_file_guides.watch_for: OTel registration errors swallowed by errorHandler mean missing metrics with no panic surfaced to callers.

anti_patterns: Adding a metric type without implementing ALL mutation methods on the wrapper.
Calling r.Registry.Register directly inside getWrappedMeter instead of returning the wrapped value — causes double-registration.
Removing reflect-based factory unwrapping — Sarama passes factories as zero-arg functions.
Initialising NewRegistry without a MetricMeter — constructor errors; unchecked means no OTel forwarding.
Using a real propagated context instead of context.Background() — go-metrics interfaces have no context parameter.

decisions.decision: Embed metrics.Registry rather than re-implementing it.
decisions.rationale: Only the write path (Register, GetOrRegister) needs interception to wrap new metrics; read/iterate/unregister stay unchanged, minimizing surface area.
Sarama emits dozens of internal metrics; callers filter noise and rename to OTel conventions without forking this package.
go-metrics mutation methods carry no context; propagating caller context is impossible without forking Sarama.

code_examples.scenario: Register a go-metrics registry forwarding to OTel with noise filtering
